response.justification: A highly experienced polyglot engineer with 15+ years of demonstrated full-stack delivery, strong TypeScript/Node.js/React alignment with the primary stack, and directly relevant AI-agent infrastructure experience in their current role (MCP code generators, agentic workflows). Testing culture is deeply embedded across the career — TDD advocacy, a testing library authored in D, and workshop facilitation. The main gaps are the absence of NestJS (the primary backend framework) and Vitest in the resume, and limited Kotlin exposure despite Java appearing in older roles. These are learnable given the breadth of the track record, but warrant probing in the interview.
strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React experience across multiple production roles, directly matching the primary stack
Active AI-agent infrastructure work at current role including MCP code generation and agentic workflow tooling
Sustained, practitioner-level TDD commitment — authored a test runner and assertion library, ran workshops, applied across varied stacks
concerns: NestJS (the core backend framework in the JD) is absent from the resume; no evidence of familiarity with its DI/module system
Vitest not mentioned; testing experience is strong but tied to other frameworks and languages
Kotlin is absent; Java appears only in older embedded/robotics contexts, not in production backend services
